subject: English
title: A Homage to our Brave Soldiers

questions.text: In the chapter, who is Ananda writing a letter to?
questions.explanation: Ananda writes a letter to Soumya after her school visit to the National War Memorial.

questions.text: Where is the National War Memorial (NWM) located?
questions.explanation: The NWM is situated near India Gate in New Delhi.

questions.text: What does the 'Amar Jawan Jyoti' represent?
options: The eternal spirit of the fallen soldiers
questions.explanation: It symbolizes the immortality of the soldiers who laid down their lives for the nation.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT one of the concentric circles at the War Memorial?
questions.explanation: The four circles are Amar (Immortality), Veerta (Bravery), Tyag (Sacrifice), and Rakshak (Protection).

questions.text: What is inscribed on the granite tablets in the Tyag Chakra?
questions.explanation: It contains the names of soldiers who were martyred in post-independence wars.

questions.text: Major Somnath Sharma was the first recipient of which gallantry award?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the PVC posthumously for his bravery in the 1947-48 war.

questions.text: The letter mentioned in the lesson written by a soldier was from:
questions.explanation: The lesson includes a moving letter from Major Padmapani Acharya to his father during the Kargil War.

questions.text: How did Ananda feel after visiting the War Memorial?
questions.explanation: The visit deeply moved her and made her realize the sacrifices made for our freedom.

questions.text: Which gallantry award is given for bravery during peace-time?
questions.explanation: Ashoka Chakra is the highest peace-time gallantry award.

questions.text: The theme of the chapter is to instill a sense of:
options: Gratitude and respect for the armed forces
questions.explanation: It aims to help students appreciate the service and sacrifice of Indian soldiers.
